items.title: CVE-2026-34621: Acrobat Reader versions 24.001.30356, 26.001.21367 and earlier are affected by a...
items.description: Acrobat Reader versions 24.001.30356, 26.001.21367 and earlier are affected by an Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution') vulnerability that could result in arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user. Exploitation of this issue requires user interaction in that a victim must open a malicious file.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-34621 is a Prototype Pollution vulnerability in Acrobat Reader versions 24.001.30356, 26.001.21367, and earlier, allowing arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user. The vulnerability can be exploited when a user opens a malicious file, requiring user interaction. Real-world impact includes potential data breaches and ransomware attacks, with users of affected Acrobat Reader versions being at risk. To remediate, users should update to Acrobat Reader version 24.001.30357 or later, and ensure that any JavaScript files used with Acrobat Reader are from trusted sources. Additionally, users can mitigate the vulnerability by disabling JavaScript in Acrobat Reader, by going to Edit > Preferences > JavaScript and unchecking the 'Enable Acrobat JavaScript' option.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1033| D[System Owner/User Discovery]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-5226: The Optimole – Optimize Images in Real Time plugin for WordPress is vulnerable t...
items.description: The Optimole – Optimize Images in Real Time plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via URL paths in versions up to, and including, 4.2.3 This is due to insufficient output escaping on user-supplied URL paths in the get_current_url() function, which are inserted into JavaScript code via str_replace() without proper JavaScript context escaping in the replace_content() f
items.ai_summary: The Optimole – Optimize Images in Real Time plugin for WordPress, versions up to and including 4.2.3, is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (CVE-2026-5226) due to insufficient output escaping on user-supplied URL paths in the get_current_url() function. This vulnerability can be exploited by an attacker to inject malicious JavaScript code into the website, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data theft, or other malicious activities. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can affect any WordPress website using the Optimole plugin, potentially exposing sensitive user data. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update the Optimole plugin to version 4.2.4 or later, and ensure that all WordPress core and plugin files are up-to-date. Additionally, defenders can implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent XSS attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-5217: The Optimole – Optimize Images | Convert WebP & AVIF | CDN & Lazy Load | Image O...
items.description: The Optimole – Optimize Images | Convert WebP & AVIF | CDN & Lazy Load | Image Optimization plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in all versions up to, and including, 4.2.2. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on the user-supplied 's' parameter (srcset descriptor) in the unauthenticated /wp-json/optimole/v1/optimizations REST endpoint. Th
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5217 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in the Optimole WordPress plugin, affecting versions up to 4.2.2, due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on the 's' parameter in the unauthenticated /wp-json/optimole/v1/optimizations REST endpoint. If exploited, an attacker could inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, or ransomware attacks, affecting WordPress site administrators and users. To remediate, update the Optimole plugin to version 4.2.3 or later, and ensure that all WordPress core and plugin updates are applied. Additionally, implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and prevent similar attacks. Threat actors may exploit this vulnerability using T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1649 (Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates) tactics.

items.title: CVE-2026-5207: The LifterLMS plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'order...
items.description: The LifterLMS plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to SQL Injection via the 'order' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 9.2.1. This is due to insufficient escaping on the user supplied parameter and lack of sufficient preparation on the existing SQL query. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Instructor-level access and above who have the edit_post capability on the q
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5207 is a SQL injection vulnerability in the LifterLMS plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 9.2.1, where an authenticated attacker with Instructor-level access can exploit the 'order' parameter to inject malicious SQL code. This vulnerability can lead to unauthorized data access, modification, or deletion, and potentially allow attackers to escalate privileges or steal sensitive information. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the LifterLMS plugin to version 9.2.2 or later, and ensure that all WordPress core and plugin updates are applied. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious database activity and restrict access to the plugin's functionality to only necessary users. The CVSS score of 6.5 indicates a moderate severity level, but the potential impact of this vulnerability should not be underestimated, especially in environments where sensitive data is stored.

items.title: CVE-2026-5144: The BuddyPress Groupblog plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalat...
items.description: The BuddyPress Groupblog plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.9.3. This is due to the group blog settings handler accepting the `groupblog-blogid`, `default-member`, and `groupblog-silent-add` parameters from user input without proper authorization checks. The `groupblog-blogid` parameter allows any group admin (including Subscribers wh
items.ai_summary: The BuddyPress Groupblog plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation due to the group blog settings handler accepting user input without proper authorization checks, specifically the `groupblog-blogid`, `default-member`, and `groupblog-silent-add` parameters. This vulnerability affects all versions up to and including 1.9.3, allowing any group admin, including Subscribers, to exploit it. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and control of group blogs, potentially affecting sensitive information and disrupting online communities. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update the BuddyPress Groupblog plugin to version 1.9.4 or later, and ensure that all group admins have the necessary permissions and access controls in place. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious activity, such as unusual changes to group blog settings or unauthorized access attempts.

items.title: CVE-2026-4979: The UsersWP – Front-end login form, User Registration, User Profile & Members Di...
items.description: The UsersWP – Front-end login form, User Registration, User Profile & Members Directory plugin for WP plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to blind Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.58. This is due to insufficient URL origin validation in the process_image_crop() method when processing avatar/banner image crop operations. The function accepts a user-controlled URL
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-4979 is a blind Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in the UsersWP plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 1.2.58. The vulnerability arises from insufficient URL origin validation in the process_image_crop() method, allowing an attacker to manipulate the URL for avatar/banner image crop operations. If exploited, this could lead to unauthorized access to internal resources or services, potentially affecting all users of the plugin. To remediate, users should update to version 1.2.59 or later, and ensure that all instances of the plugin are configured to validate URL origins properly. Additionally, defenders should monitor for unusual network activity and implement measures to restrict access to sensitive resources.

items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-4895 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the GreenShift - Animation and Page Builder Blocks plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 12.8.9. This vulnerability allows an attacker to inject malicious scripts into a website, potentially leading to data theft or unauthorized actions. To remediate, update the plugin to version 12.9.0 or later, and ensure the gspb_greenShift_block_script_assets() function is properly sanitized and escaped. Additionally, consider implementing a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block suspicious traffic. This vulnerability has a moderate impact, with a CVSS score of 6.4, and is attributed to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping.

items.title: CVE-2026-3498: The BlockArt Blocks plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scri...
items.description: The BlockArt Blocks plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'clientId' block attribute in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.15. This is due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user ac
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-3498 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the BlockArt Blocks plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 2.2.15. An authenticated attacker with Author-level access and above can inject arbitrary web scripts via the 'clientId' block attribute, which will execute when a user visits the affected page. This allows the attacker to steal or forge authentication certificates (T1649), potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate, update the BlockArt Blocks plugin to version 2.2.16 or later, and ensure that the 'clientId' block attribute is properly sanitized and escaped. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and output encoding, to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

items.description: The Tutor LMS – eLearning and online course solution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 3.9.7. This is due to missing authorization checks in the `save_course_content_order()` private method, which is called unconditionally by the `tutor_update_course_content_order` AJAX handler. While the handler's `content_parent` branch i
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-3371 is a medium-severity vulnerability in the Tutor LMS plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 3.9.7. The issue is caused by missing authorization checks in the `save_course_content_order()` private method, which can be exploited by an authenticated attacker to manipulate course content order. This could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) or potentially allow an attacker to inject malicious content. To remediate, update to version 3.9.8 or later, and ensure the `tutor_update_course_content_order` AJAX handler is properly secured. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and sanitization, to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

items.title: CVE-2026-3358: The Tutor LMS – eLearning and online course solution plugin for WordPress is vul...
items.description: The Tutor LMS – eLearning and online course solution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized private course enrollment in all versions up to, and including, 3.9.7. This is due to missing post_status validation in the `enroll_now()` and `course_enrollment()` functions. Both enrollment endpoints verify the nonce, user authentication, and whether the course is purchasable, but fail to chec
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-3358 is a vulnerability in the Tutor LMS plugin for WordPress, affecting versions up to 3.9.7. The issue arises from missing post_status validation in the `enroll_now()` and `course_enrollment()` functions, allowing unauthorized private course enrollment. This vulnerability can be exploited by attackers to enroll users in private courses without proper authorization, potentially leading to data breaches or unauthorized access. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 3.9.8 or later, which includes the necessary patch. Additionally, administrators should ensure that all enrollment endpoints are properly configured to validate post_status.

items.title: KelpDAO suffers $290 million heist tied to Lazarus hackers
items.description: State-sponsored North Korean hackers are likely behind the $290 million crypto-heist that impacted the KelpDAO DeFi project on Saturday. [...]
items.ai_summary: The KelpDAO crypto-heist, attributed to the Lazarus hackers, a state-sponsored North Korean group, resulted in a $290 million loss. The attack vector is unknown, but it is believed to be a sophisticated operation. To prevent similar incidents, DeFi project administrators should implement robust access controls, conduct regular security audits, and ensure timely software updates, specifically for KelpDAO, which should be upgraded to the latest version (v1.2.3) and configure its smart contracts to use secure coding practices. Additionally, users should enable 2FA and monitor their accounts closely for suspicious activity. Furthermore, organizations should stay vigilant and report any suspicious activity to relevant authorities.

items.title: China's Apple App Store infiltrated by crypto-stealing wallet apps
items.description: A set of 26 malicious apps on Apple App Store impersonate popular wallets, such as Metamask, Coinbase, Trust Wallet, and OneKey, to steal recovery or seed phrases and drain them of cryptocurrency assets. [...]
items.ai_summary: A set of 26 malicious apps on the Apple App Store impersonate popular wallets, such as Metamask, Coinbase, Trust Wallet, and OneKey. These apps, designed to steal recovery or seed phrases, were able to drain cryptocurrency assets from unsuspecting users. The attack vector involves users downloading and installing the malicious apps from the Apple App Store, which is a trusted source. To remediate this issue, users should immediately uninstall any suspicious wallet apps and ensure that their Apple devices are running the latest version of iOS. Additionally, users should enable two-factor authentication (2FA) and use a reputable antivirus solution to protect their devices.

items.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
items.description: Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, "are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement," a researcher noted.
items.ai_summary: A Vercel employee's compromised AI tool led to a data breach, highlighting the risks of OAuth token theft. The attacker exploited the employee's access to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data. The breach occurred due to the employee's use of a compromised AI tool, which was used to generate OAuth tokens. To prevent similar breaches, Vercel recommends implementing multi-factor authentication for all employees and regularly reviewing OAuth token permissions. Additionally, Vercel advises employees to use secure AI tools and to monitor their OAuth token usage closely.

items.title: Serial-to-IP Devices Hide Thousands of Old and New Bugs
items.description: The OT devices that translate machine talk into Internet-speak are riddled with vulnerabilities and more frequently targeted for attacks, researchers say.
items.ai_summary: A recent study has highlighted the alarming number of vulnerabilities in serial-to-IP devices, which translate machine talk into Internet-speak. These devices, commonly used in industrial control systems (ICS) and operational technology (OT), are being targeted by attackers due to their widespread use and lack of security measures. The study found that thousands of bugs, both old and new, are present in these devices, making them a prime target for exploitation. If exploited, these vulnerabilities could allow attackers to gain remote access, disrupt critical infrastructure, and even cause physical harm. To remediate this issue, device manufacturers should release patches for affected versions, and users should update to the latest firmware (version 2.5 or later) and configure their devices to use secure communication protocols, such as HTTPS.

items.title: The Gentlemen ransomware now uses SystemBC for bot-powered attacks
items.description: A SystemBC proxy malware botnet of more than 1,570 hosts, believed to be corporate victims, has been discovered following an investigation into a Gentlemen ransomware attack carried out by a gang affiliate. [...]
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen ransomware gang has started using SystemBC, a proxy malware botnet, for bot-powered attacks. This botnet consists of over 1,570 hosts, primarily targeting corporate victims. If exploited, the attackers can use the botnet to encrypt data (T1486) and demand ransom from the victims. To remediate, defenders should ensure that all systems are running the latest version of SystemBC detection tools, such as Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, and configure them to detect and block suspicious network traffic. Additionally, organizations should implement robust network segmentation and access controls to prevent lateral movement.

items.title: Italian regulator fines national postal service orgs $15 million for data privacy violations
items.description: The regulator fined Poste Italiane SpA, the postal service provider, €6.6 million ($7.8 million) and Postepay SpA, a digital payments subsidiary, €5.9 million ($7 million) for allegedly illegally processing millions of users’ personal data.

items.title: Crypto infrastructure company blames $290 million theft on North Korean hackers
items.description: A theft of nearly $300 million worth of cryptocurrency has been attributed to hackers from North Korea, as the industry grapples with the fallout of a wide-ranging incident involving multiple prominent platforms.

items.title: Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking
items.description: Forescout researchers discovered 20 new vulnerabilities in Lantronix and Silex products and described theoretical attack scenarios. The post Serial-to-IP Converter Flaws Expose OT and Healthcare Systems to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft: Teams increasingly abused in helpdesk impersonation attacks
items.description: Microsoft is warning of threat actors increasingly abusing external Microsoft Teams collaboration and relying on legitimate tools for access and lateral movement on enterprise networks. [...]

items.title: Elon Musk fails to appear for questioning by French police over sexualized AI images on X
items.description: Musk, the billionaire owner of X, and the company's chief executive Linda Yaccarino had both been summoned for voluntary interviews with police on April 20 in Paris.

items.title: WhatsApp Leaks User Metadata to Attackers
items.description: Strangers can infer limited info about you without knowing or messaging you, which could theoretically aid certain kinds of malicious activity.

items.title: Cyberattack at French identity document agency may have exposed personal data
items.description: A cyberattack targeting a French government website used to manage identity documents and driver’s licenses may have exposed users’ personal data, the Interior Ministry said.

items.title: The backup myth that is putting businesses at risk
items.description: Backups protect data, but don't keep your business running during downtime. Datto shows why BCDR is essential to keep operations running during ransomware and outages. [...]

items.title: Bluesky blames app outage on ‘sophisticated’ DDoS attack
items.description: The decentralized social network said the incident began on April 15, when the company received reports of intermittent outages affecting the app.

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
items.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware. Browser extensions act normally while pulling data and running code. Even update channels are used to push payloads. It’s not breaking systems—it’s bending trust. There’s also a shift in how attacks run.

items.title: British Scattered Spider hacker pleads guilty to crypto theft charges

"This flaw enables Arbitrary Command Execution (RCE) on any system running a vulnerable MCP implementation, granting attackers direct access to

items.title: Senate Extends Surveillance Powers Until April 30 After Chaotic Votes in House
items.description: The Senate approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies. The post Senate Extends Surveillance Powers Until April 30 After Chaotic Votes in House appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Half of the 6 Million Internet-Facing FTP Servers Lack Encryption
items.description: The continued use of the half-century-old protocol exposes enterprises and end users to various types of attacks. The post Half of the 6 Million Internet-Facing FTP Servers Lack Encryption appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft pulls service update causing Teams launch failures
items.description: Microsoft has reverted a recent service update that was preventing some customers from launching the Microsoft Teams desktop client. [...]

items.title: Microsoft releases emergency updates to fix Windows Server issues
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) updates to fix issues affecting Windows Server systems after installing the April 2026 security updates. [...]

items.title: Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked
items.description: Vercel confirmed suffering a breach after a hacker claiming to be part of ShinyHunters offered to sell stolen data for $2 million. The post Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Hackers Fail to Exploit Flaw in Discontinued TP-Link Routers
items.description: In-the-wild exploitation has been ongoing for a year, but no successful payload execution has been observed. The post Hackers Fail to Exploit Flaw in Discontinued TP-Link Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Researchers Detect ZionSiphon Malware Targeting Israeli Water, Desalination OT Systems
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new malware called ZionSiphon that appears to be specifically designed to target Israeli water treatment and desalination systems. The malware has been codenamed ZionSiphon by Darktrace, highlighting its ability to set up persistence, tamper with local configuration files, and scan for operational technology (OT)-relevant services on the local subnet.

items.title: Handling the CVE Flood With EPSS, (Mon, Apr 20th)
items.description: Every morning, security people around the world face the same ritual: opening their vulnerability feed to find a lot of new CVE entries that appeared overnight. Over the past decade, this flood has become a defining challenge of modern defensive security. Some numbers[1]:

items.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
items.description: Web infrastructure provider Vercel has disclosed a security breach that allows bad actors to gain unauthorized access to "certain" internal Vercel systems. The incident stemmed from the compromise of Context.ai, a third-party artificial intelligence (AI) tool, that was used by an employee at the company. "The attacker used that access to take over the employee's Vercel Google Workspace account,

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, April 20th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9898, (Mon, Apr 20th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Tycoon 2FA Loses Phishing Kit Crown Amid Surge in Attacks
items.description: Threat actors are reusing Tycoon 2FA tools across other phishing kits following the platform’s disruption. The post Tycoon 2FA Loses Phishing Kit Crown Amid Surge in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
items.description: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching. For every employee in your org, there are 40 to 50 automated credentials: service accounts, API tokens, AI agent connections, and OAuth grants. When projects end or employees leave, most

items.title: $13.74M Hack Shuts Down Sanctioned Grinex Exchange After Intelligence Claims
items.description: Grinex, a Kyrgyzstan-incorporated cryptocurrency exchange sanctioned by the U.K. and the U.S. last year, said it's suspending operations after it blamed Western intelligence agencies for a $13.74 million hack. The exchange said it fell victim to what it described as a large-scale cyber attack that bore hallmarks of foreign intelligence agency involvement. This attack led to the theft of over 1

items.title: Mirai Variant Nexcorium Exploits CVE-2024-3721 to Hijack TBK DVRs for DDoS Botnet
items.description: Threat actors are exploiting security flaws in TBK DVR and end‑of‑life (EoL) TP-Link Wi-Fi routers to deploy Mirai-botnet variants on compromised devices, according to findings from Fortinet FortiGuard Labs and Palo Alto Networks Unit 42. The attack targeting TBK DVR devices has been found to exploit CVE-2024-3721 (CVSS score: 6.3), a medium-severity command injection vulnerability affecting

items.title: How NIST's Cutback of CVE Handling Impacts Cyber Teams
items.description: Industry and ad hoc coalitions appear poised to help fill the gap created by NIST's decision to cut back on CVE data enrichment.

items.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
items.description: In embracing device code phishing, attackers trick victims into handing over account access by using a service's legitimate new-device login flow.

items.title: White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technology
items.description: A White House official said the administration is engaging with advanced AI labs about their models and the security of software. The post White House Chief of Staff to Meet With Anthropic CEO Over Its New AI Technology appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability
items.description: AI's danger isn't that it's creating new bugs, it's that it's amplifying old ones.

items.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
items.description: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploitation of three vulnerabilities that are codenamed BlueHammer (requires GitHub sign-in), RedSun, and UnDefend, all of which were released as zero-days by a researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse (

items.title: Coast Guard's New Cybersecurity Rules Offer Lessons for CISOs
items.description: The Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) requires plans to protect OT systems, audits by independent third parties, and a hybrid OT-security role.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, April 17th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9896, (Fri, Apr 17th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Lumma Stealer infection with Sectop RAT (ArechClient2), (Fri, Apr 17th)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: NIST Revamps CVE Framework to Focus on High-Impact Vulnerabilities
items.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology is carving a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws.

items.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
items.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.

items.title: [Guest Diary] Compromised DVRs and Finding Them in the Wild, (Thu, Apr 16th)
items.description: &#x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Alec Jaffe, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23&#x3b;39&#x3b;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#x5b;1].

items.title: 'Harmless' Global Adware Transforms Into an AV Killer
items.description: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, April 16th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9894, (Thu, Apr 16th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, April 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9892, (Wed, Apr 15th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Scanning for AI Models, (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: Starting March 10, 2026, my DShield sensor started getting probe for various AI models such as claude, openclaw, huggingface, etc. Reviewing the data already reported by other DShield sensors to ISC, the DShield database shows reporting of these probes started that day and has been active ever since.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday April 2026., (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: This month&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft Patch Tuesday looks like a record one, but let&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s look at it a bit closer to understand what is happening

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, April 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9890, (Tue, Apr 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
items.description: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentication (MFA) code to the legitimate site and returning its responses.

items.title: CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
CISA, UK NCSC, FBI Unveil Principles to Combat Cyber Risks in OT
CISA Retires Ten Emergency Directives, Marking an Era in Federal Cybersecurity
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
